description: Changsha Historical and Cultural District, preserving traditional architectural styles and gathering Changsha's signature snacks.
A millennium-old academy, one of China's four great academies, and the birthplace of Huxiang culture.
A millennium-old academy, a renowned higher education institution in China with a beautiful campus environment.
An artificial island in the heart of the lake, a model of modern urban planning, ideal for lakeside walks to enjoy scenic views of the lake and mountains.
The largest public photography art museum in the country, regularly hosting photography exhibitions.
A popular retro-themed restaurant known for its old Changsha street-style decor and signature spicy crayfish.
A professional museum showcasing Hunan's geological resources and mineral specimens.
A famous time-honored restaurant in Changsha, representing Xiang cuisine and the birthplace of snacks like stinky tofu.
A natural islet in the Xiang River, home to the Youth Mao Zedong Statue and a landmark attraction in Changsha.
A national first-class museum featuring artifacts from the Mawangdui Han Tombs, showcasing Hunan's historical and cultural heritage.
The largest comprehensive park in Changsha, an important site for commemorating revolutionary martyrs.
The former residence of Jia Yi, a renowned Western Han politician, a well-preserved ancient scholar's home.
A reconstructed ancient-style building dedicated to Du Fu, offering views of the Xiang River at night.
One of China's four famous pavilions, the setting for Du Mu's poem line 'Stopping my carriage, I love the maple woods in the evening.'
A famous mountain in Changsha with rich historical and cultural significance, including attractions like Yuelu Academy.
